Making ASCII art in Vim

Published 2026-07-12
Last edited 2026-07-21

I like using Vim to make ASCII art. I don't use any plugins; Vim already has a bunch of built-in features that are super useful for making ASCII art!

     _  _/_Z  _
    / |// / |/ \
    \__/_/_/_/_/

This article is for people who have made ASCII art before and want to see what other tools are out there. Instead, just open up your favorite text editor and start typing.

Move your cursor past the end of the line

Vim normally doesn't allow you to move your cursor past the end of the line. To change this, run the following command:

:set virtualedit=all

Parting words

You don't need to know any tricks to make good ASCII art. If you've decided to make art, a lack of tools isn't going to stop you.
